title: [1.18] Flaky integration test: daprd/workflow/crossapp/pending/remotewithtimer fails on macos-14 amd64 with placement health check connection refused
body:
## In what area(s)?

`area/test/integration`, `area/runtime` (placement service startup), darwin/macos-14 amd64 runner.

## What version of Dapr?

Edge (master) — observed in CI run https://github.com/dapr/dapr/actions/runs/25680478732/job/75391247807 (PR #9731).

## Expected Behavior

`Test_Integration/daprd/workflow/crossapp/pending/remotewithtimer/run` should consistently pass. The placement service spawned by the test framework should bind to its assigned port and serve `/healthz` before `place.WaitUntilRunning` times out.

## Actual Behavior

The placement service never starts (or never binds its port) on the macos-14 amd64 runner. Both `place.WaitUntilRunning` and the dependent daprd's `/healthz` poll fail.

```
=== FAIL: Test_Integration/daprd/workflow/crossapp/pending/remotewithtimer/run (55.96s)
    remotewithtimer.go:111:
        Error Trace: tests/integration/framework/process/placement/placement.go:161
        Error:       Received unexpected error:
                     Get "http://127.0.0.1:6371/healthz": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:6371: connect: connection refused
    remotewithtimer.go:111:
        Error:       Condition never satisfied
        Test:        Test_Integration/daprd/workflow/crossapp/pending/remotewithtimer/run
    remotewithtimer.go:112:
        Error Trace: tests/integration/framework/process/daprd/daprd.go:235
        Error:       Received unexpected error:
                     Get "http://127.0.0.1:6381/v1.0/healthz": context deadline exceeded
```

Followed by daprd's own retry loop logging repeated `Failed to connect to placement service ... dial tcp 127.0.0.1:6370: connect: connection refused. Retrying...` until the test deadline.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Push a commit to a PR.
2. Wait for the `Integration tests (macos-14, darwin, amd64)` job to flake.
3. Observe the failure on `remotewithtimer` (placement service health-check is the first signal).

## Hypothesis

The test framework launches the placement service as a child process and immediately polls `/healthz`. On macos-14 amd64 runners (which are emulated x86_64 atop the Apple Silicon hosts GitHub now ships), placement startup is intermittently slow or the binary fails to bind without surfacing an error. The dependent daprd then can't connect to placement and times out via its retry loop.

Two symptoms suggest the placement binary is genuinely down (not just slow):
1. `/healthz` returns `connection refused`, not a timeout — the listener never opened.
2. daprd's retry loop keeps getting `connection refused` for the full run duration (~55s), so this isn't a brief window — the placement process really never came up on this run.

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. Retry `placement.Run` once on startup failure on darwin amd64 (similar to how some tests treat the actor-not-hosted post-restart window).
2. Surface the placement process's stderr/stdout when health-check fails so the next failure mode is diagnosable, not opaque.
3. Investigate why placement intermittently fails to bind its listener on macos-14 amd64 — could be tied to GitHub's emulated-runner backing.

This is unrelated to PR #9731 (MCPServer feature) — `remotewithtimer.go` doesn't touch MCP code.

## Release Note

NONE
